summary: Completed comprehensive testing of AI Video Service (VidFlux AI). Backend API tests passed 13/15 with 2 intermittent 502 errors during high load. Frontend UI loads correctly but has rendering issues with video data.

minor.issue: Intermittent 502 errors under load (2 of 15 tests)
minor.note: API works fine on retry - likely related to test execution timing

ui_bugs.issue: Video page shows loading spinner indefinitely for completed videos - video element not rendered even though API returns completed status with video_url
Video gallery shows empty placeholder cards instead of completed videos - API call succeeds but videos state not populated correctly

integration_issues.issue: Videos from /api/videos not rendering in gallery - API returns data but React state not updating
Video with poster_url shows loading spinner instead of completed video player

passed_tests: API /api/ root endpoint returns VidFlux AI message
API /api/formats returns all 10 video formats including chat_animation
API /api/video/generate accepts format_id='auto' and starts job
API /api/video/{id} returns completed video with poster_url
Auto-detection identifies chat_animation format from dialog prompt
Homepage loads with hero image and navigation
Create page loads with space background and 100 stars
Submit button enables when prompt is filled
Tab buttons present (Все, Видео, Фото, Недавние)
'Сделать видео' and 'Сделать фото' buttons present and clickable
action_items: CRITICAL: Fix VideoPage.jsx - video element not rendered for completed videos. Issue is in isLoading/isCompleted state handling or data not being set correctly after API response
CRITICAL: Fix HomePage.jsx - videos array state not updating from /api/videos response. Gallery shows empty cards instead of completed video thumbnails with posters
Review axios configuration in frontend - API calls may be timing out or erroring silently
Consider adding error boundary and console logging for API call debugging
critical_code_review_comments: VideoPage.jsx line 21: setIsLoading(false) is called but isProcessing check (line 79) might still be true causing wrong rendering path
HomePage.jsx line 34-35: videos state is set but filter for completedVideos (line 48) might have timing issue with async state update

success_rate.frontend: 60% (UI loads but video data display fails)

mocked_apis_list: yt-dlp YouTube download - uses colored placeholder instead due to blocking

context_for_next_testing_agent: Backend APIs are fully working (tested via curl and pytest). Frontend has rendering issues - the API calls return data but React components don't display it. Focus debugging on: 1) Check browser console for errors when loading VideoPage, 2) Verify axios is correctly configured and making successful API calls, 3) Debug React state updates in useEffect hooks of HomePage.jsx and VideoPage.jsx
